steps.title: Set up the quadratic equation
steps.content: The product of $x$ and $(x-13)$ equals $378$: $$x(x-13)=378 \implies x^2-13x-378=0.$$

steps.title: Solve and identify x
steps.content: Factor: $(x-27)(x+14)=0$, giving $x=27$ or $x=-14$. Since $x$ is positive, $x=27$. The answer is 27.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: As a final check, make sure the computed value answers exactly what the question asks for. The work above gives $27$; substituting or interpreting it in the original condition satisfies the requirement, so $27$ is the value to enter.
